channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news curated from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: 🇮🇷 Trump Rejects Iranian Proposal to Reopen Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump told security officials he is unlikely to accept Iran's offer to reopen the Strait of Hormuz in exchange for ending the US blockade without addressing nuclear negotiations. Brent crude remains above $111 as the stalemate continues.

item.title: 🇩🇪 German Chancellor Says US Being Humiliated by Iran Negotiations
item.description: Friedrich Merz criticized US handling of Iran negotiations, saying Tehran is negotiating very skillfully and that the US is being humiliated in the conflict. The comments highlight growing European concerns about transatlantic coordination.

item.title: 🇰🇵 North Korea ICBMs Can Strike US Mainland, Pentagon Official Warns
item.description: A senior US defense official confirmed North Korea has the capability to strike the US mainland with intercontinental ballistic missiles, underscoring the growing ballistic missile threat amid Pyongyang's diplomatic outreach to Trump.

item.title: 💰 Global Military Spending Hits Record $4 Trillion
item.description: Global defense expenditure reached a historic high of $4 trillion, with the United States, China, and Russia accounting for more than half of total spending, according to new research reflecting intensified great power competition.

item.title: 🇪🇺⚔️ NATO weaknesses exposed by Iran war, analysis finds
item.description: A Politico EU analysis identifies five key ways the Iran conflict reveals NATO is unprepared to fight Russia, from munitions depletion to alliance coordination gaps.

item.title: 🛢️🚢 Hormuz shipping traffic remains muted as US-Iran talks stall
item.description: Only 7 ships crossed the Strait in 24 hours, a fraction of the 140 daily passages before the war. Data shows Iran has moved oil through the US blockade while negotiations remain deadlocked.

item.title: 🇨🇦💰 Canada launches sovereign wealth fund with lower deficit
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney announced Canada's first sovereign wealth fund with an initial Cbn contribution. The deficit is now 14% lower than projected, aided by higher oil prices. The fund will invest in energy, infrastructure, mining, and technology.

item.title: ⚖️🇦🇫 Jury deliberates in trial of IS militant over Kabul airport bombing
item.description: A Virginia jury began deliberations in the trial of Mohammad Sharifullah, an alleged Islamic State militant charged with helping plan the 2021 Abbey Gate suicide bombing that killed 13 US service members and 160 Afghans during the US withdrawal from Afghanistan.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump approval rating sinks to record low as Iran war drives cost-of-living concerns
item.description: President Trump's approval rating has fallen to the lowest level of his second term, with Americans citing rising gasoline prices and economic strain from the Iran war as key factors in their dissatisfaction.

item.title: 🇦🇪 UAE exits OPEC and OPEC+ in major blow to oil producer alliance
item.description: The United Arab Emirates announced it is quitting both OPEC and OPEC+ effective May 1, dealing a heavy blow to the oil exporting groups at a time when the Iran war has caused an historic energy shock.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Bank of Japan holds rates steady but three board members dissent, call for hike
item.description: The BOJ kept interest rates unchanged but three of nine board members proposed hiking borrowing costs, signaling concern over inflationary pressures from the Middle East conflict affecting Japan's economy.

item.title: 🇬🇧 Russian shadow fleet undeterred by UK threats, nearly 100 ships cross British waters
item.description: A Reuters analysis shows that Prime Minister Keir Starmer's decision to let the British military board Russian shadow fleet ships has had no clear impact, with nearly 100 vessels still passing through UK waters.

item.title: 🇺🇦🔫 Ukraine shuts down arms network supplying weapons to pro-Russian figures including Steven Seagal
item.description: Ukrainian authorities dismantled an arms trafficking network that funneled weapons from occupied territories and Slovakia to pro-Russian figures, including actor Steven Seagal.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇺🇦🏛️ McConnell criticizes Pentagon for withholding M in Ukraine aid
item.description: Former Senate GOP Leader Mitch McConnell criticized the Pentagon for sitting on million in congressionally approved Ukraine aid, highlighting tensions over military support.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇺🏛️ Senate rejects bid to curb Trump military action in Cuba
item.description: The Senate voted down legislation that would have barred US military action against Cuba without congressional approval, maintaining presidential war powers flexibility.

item.title: 🌾 Ukraine Confronts Israel Over Purchase of Russian-Stolen Grain
item.description: President Zelenskiy condemned Israel for buying grain from occupied Ukrainian territory that he says was stolen by Russia, threatening sanctions and sparking a diplomatic row.

item.title: 📈 US Bond Markets Split as Middle East Conflict Tests Fed Policy Outlook
item.description: The Iran war has fractured US fixed-income markets, with corporate credit investors pricing in peace while Treasury holders remain focused on inflation risks that could limit Federal Reserve easing.

item.title: 💰 Major US Companies Project Resilience Despite Mounting Iran War Risks
item.description: Top American firms from GM to Coca-Cola are reassuring investors they can weather Iran war fallout, even as surging fuel and packaging costs threaten profit margins.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US Ambassador to Ukraine Steps Down Amid Stalled Peace Talks
item.description: Acting US Ambassador Julie Davis will retire and leave her Kyiv post in June, as US-brokered ceasefire negotiations with Russia remain deadlocked.

item.title: 🛸 Drone Diplomacy Wins Ukraine Allies, But Now It Must Deliver
item.description: Zelenskiy has leveraged Ukraine drone warfare expertise into diplomatic deals across Middle East and Europe, using military prowess to boost Kyiv international clout.

item.title: 🔥 Ukrainian Drone Strike Sparks Fire at Russian Tuapse Refinery
item.description: A Ukrainian drone attack caused a major fire at a Russian oil refinery in Tuapse, with Putin citing the incident as evidence of intensified Ukrainian attacks on civilian infrastructure.

item.title: 🚢 Russian Superyacht Allowed Through Blocked Hormuz Strait
item.description: A superyacht owned by sanctioned Russian billionaire Alexey Mordashov transited the blockaded Strait of Hormuz after neither Iran nor the US objected, highlighting selective enforcement of the wartime closure.

item.title: 🛢️ First Japanese Oil Tanker Crosses Hormuz Since Iran War Began
item.description: The Idemitsu Maru, carrying 2 million barrels of Saudi crude, became the first Japan-linked tanker to successfully navigate the blockaded Strait of Hormuz since the conflict started.

item.title: ⚓ US Military Boards Cargo Ship During Iran Blockade, Releases It
item.description: The US military boarded a commercial vessel suspected of heading to Iran during the ongoing blockade but released it after inspection, marking a departure from previous interdictions.

item.title: 🚫 Peace Talks at Impasse as Trump Rejects Latest Iran Proposal
item.description: President Trump expressed dissatisfaction with Iran latest ceasefire proposal, saying Tehran informed the US it is in a state of collapse and figuring out its leadership situation.
